mnemonics: A-C-E: The three common subclasses - Abstract, Concrete, and Extends
keyPoints: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated and may contain abstract methods.
Concrete classes are fully implemented and can be instantiated.
Subclasses use the 'extends' keyword to inherit from a superclass.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to implement all abstract methods from an abstract superclass.
Using 'implements' instead of 'extends' when inheriting from a class.
Trying to instantiate an abstract class directly with the 'new' keyword.
